orgPolicy.title: FY27 Offer Calculators Now Live
orgPolicy.summary: Updated offer calculators reflecting variable comp are live for all levels. Use these for all new offers going forward.
bullets: L6 variable target increased to 25% (up from 20%)
L1–L5: file is manual — enter variable $ or % depending on offer type
Hourly (L1–L5): $1,250 flat target | Salary L1–L3: 5% | L4: 7.5% | L5: 10%
Calculators saved in the Invite Drive folder

tp.point: FY27 offer calculators are updated — use these for all offers now. L6 variable is now 25%; L1–L5 requires manual entry.

orgPolicy.title: Comp Sheets Interim Workaround
orgPolicy.summary: Comp sheets are temporarily unavailable. Recruiters can view comp ranges in Greenhouse job details. PEs have a master spreadsheet for triage.
bullets: View comp range directly in Greenhouse job details (Loom walkthrough available)
For NHG and other info not in GH, ping your PE
PEs: use master spreadsheet for triage — do not share with recruiters directly

tp.point: Comp sheets are temporarily down. View comp ranges in Greenhouse job details — PE has a walkthrough Loom. Ping your PE for anything you can't find.

orgPolicy.title: Scorecards Must Include Human-Written Summary
orgPolicy.summary: Scorecards submitted to Hiring Committee must include a human-authored summary — not just BrightHire/AI-generated transcription. Missing summaries are flagged and delay HC submissions.
bullets: Every scorecard needs a human-led synthesis — AI tools support, not replace, the interviewer's voice
Transcription-only scorecards will be flagged in Hiring Committee
Do NOT submit to HC if a scorecard is missing its human summary — a delayed submission is better than an incomplete one

tp.point: Scorecards sent to HC must have a human-written summary — BrightHire transcripts alone are not sufficient. Hold interviewers accountable.

orgPolicy.title: New Variable Cash Award Field in Greenhouse Offers
orgPolicy.summary: New 'Are they receiving a % Variable Cash Award?' field is now live in Greenhouse offer details. Must be completed for all offers.
bullets: Three options: No - Hourly/Not Eligible | Yes - US Candidate | Yes - CANADA Candidate
Selecting 'Yes' reveals a % field — variable targets: L1–L3: 5%, L4: 7.5%, L5: 10%
50% individual / 50% company performance split
Always double-check offer details before sending

tp.point: New VC field in Greenhouse offer details — select the right option (Hourly, US, or Canada) and enter the % if applicable.

orgPolicy.title: VMA Evolution Launching August 3
orgPolicy.summary: Gusto's VMA program is being refreshed and relaunched August 3. Updated question bank, rubric, and a new AI mindset component. Celena joins next PE meeting to walk through recruiter actions.
bullets: Updated question bank, calibrated rubric, new AI mindset component (learning/adaptability mindset — not technical skills)
Nomination: I&O partners with Invite PEs via structured process; self-volunteering replaced
Performance tie-in: VMA participation named as formal contribution for L3–L6 Gusties
Engineering: standalone VMAs reintroduced immediately; CX: full reintroduction targeted by Q2
Celena joining next PE meeting to cover what Invite needs to do — flag your calendar

tp.point: VMA is getting a major refresh launching August 3. More details on recruiter actions coming from your PE after Celena's session.

orgPolicy.title: Onboarding Location Field Updated in Greenhouse
orgPolicy.summary: PeopleTech updated the onboarding location field so data flows correctly into Workday. New standardized options replace previous free-text.
bullets: New options: Remote, San Francisco, New York, Denver
Ensure onboarding location is accurate — feeds directly to the Onboarding team

tp.point: Onboarding location field in Greenhouse now has standardized options: Remote, SF, NY, Denver. Use these going forward.

orgPolicy.title: Gusto Closed June 19 (Juneteenth)
orgPolicy.summary: Gusto is closed this Friday, June 19th for Juneteenth. Plan accordingly for candidate communications and offer timelines.
bullets: No Gusto business day on Friday, June 19

tp.point: Heads up: Gusto is closed this Friday, June 19 for Juneteenth.

items.heading: New InviteOS Features Now Live
bullets: Pending Actions: centralized view of candidate emails, scorecards, and referrals awaiting your review
Submit Feedback button: in-app button to send bugs/ideas directly to People Tech Jira queue
Loom walkthrough available (link in source)
Beta testers wanted for Debrief & Hiring Committee Agent — reply in #invite-team thread to join

items.heading: IOPE Cycle Focus: Auto-Population & CA Relocation Language
bullets: Invite Ops + PeopleTech working on auto-populating job-related fields in offer details — less manual work coming
California Bill 692: Invite Ops + Legal + Comp aligning on relocation agreement language for CA-bound hires — details coming soon
IKB content actively being migrated to Notion

items.heading: Cycle 2 Is Live (June 15 – July 31)
bullets: Cycle 2 launched today — IOPE working with DRIs to finalize initiative pages
If not done by June 13 deadline: fill in 'What is this?' and 'Done when...' fields in your Notion initiative pages
Use the Invite Cycle Planner Claude skill or edit Notion directly

items.heading: Gabe Cadavillo Departing Gusto
bullets: Gabe Cadavillo (GTM team) is leaving Gusto at the end of this week after 4+ years
Leaving in great standing

items.heading: Greenhouse Open for Ops Conference Takeaways
bullets: Lisa Pham and Alli Belamide attended — theme: TA ops built to flex
Build flexible frameworks, not rigid solutions; enablement must be ongoing (not one-time training)
Metrics rethink: TTF is a lagging indicator; QoH belongs to HMs (Invite owns Quality of Submittal); activity metrics = coaching tool
Human-in-the-loop tiers: 100% human for judgment/experience; AI-assisted for screening/synthesis; fully automated for status updates
GH products to watch: Fraud detection, Notetaker, Analytics, Ezra (AI screening)

items.heading: Requisition Approval Process Review — Next PE Meeting
bullets: Saumya Chopra (People Tech) joining next Wednesday's PE meeting
Topics: multi-step requisition approval process and requisition routing by job details
Goal: hear pain points from recruiters and prioritize People Tech fixes

items.heading: People Staff Passdowns (Week of June 9)
bullets: Cycle 2 alignment: sharpen focus on AI-native work and modernizing People service delivery; clear ownership, sequenced priorities
Office experience survey results: top friction = conference room availability, desk predictability, noise/lighting, commute. Near-term: anchor day nudges, space improvements
People Partner charter Sprint 1 underway: org health metrics standardization, ER service delivery, redesigned talent review framework
